perturbation.original_text: (vi) "Program Content", with respect to this Agreement, shall mean all program content set forth in Exhibit 1 to which Party A owns Internet and media copyrights and which are required for Party B Business, including but not limited to programs on news, policy trends, entertainment, business and economic trends.
perturbation.changed_text: (vi) "Program Content", with respect to this Agreement, shall mean only the news programs explicitly listed by name in Exhibit 1 to which Party A owns Internet and media copyrights and which are required for Party B Business. General categories of programming such as 'entertainment' or 'business and economic trends' are explicitly excluded.
perturbation.explanation: This change restricts the definition of "Program Content" to only news programs explicitly listed in Exhibit 1, excluding broader categories. This contradicts the original definition which included all program content set forth in Exhibit 1 including 'entertainment' or 'business and economic trends'. This in-text contradiction affects the scope of content Party B is licensed to use.
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE THREE SERVICE SCOPE AND METHOD OF PROVISION
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both Parties agree that Party A shall license the Program Content required in Party B Business to Party B, and Party B shall accept the services provided by Party A, to the extent, at the time or times, and in the manner as agreed to by the Parties herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of "Program Content" to only explicitly listed news programs, excluding broader categories such as 'entertainment' or 'business and economic trends'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states 'Party A shall license the Program Content required in Party B Business to Party B', because the scope of 'Program Content' is now significantly restricted, making it unclear if Party A is obligated to license program content from entertainment and business/economic trends, creating a direct and undeniable contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Both Parties acknowledge and agree that with respect to Program Content licensed to Party B hereunder, Party B shall not have any copyright or any other Intellectual Property Right. If Party B obtains any Intellectual Property Right in respect of the Program Content during its use of the same, Party B shall notify Party A and, upon its request in writing, sign all documents and take all actions required to assign such Intellectual Property Right to Party A, and ensure the Intellectual Property Right so obtained by Party A is legitimate, complete, and free from any encumbrance.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Both Parties acknowledge and agree that with respect to Program Content licensed to Party B hereunder, Party B retains the right to create derivative works and shall own the copyright to these derivative works. If Party B obtains any Intellectual Property Right in respect of the Program Content during its use of the same, the ownership will be shared between the parties by 50%.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original clause which stated that Party B shall not have any copyright or any other Intellectual Property Right in the licensed Program Content. Now, Party B is allowed to create derivative works and own it as well as share the Intellectual Property ownership by 50%. This creates a direct contradiction about the intellectual property ownership of the Program Content.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE FIVE INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS TO THE PROGRAM CONTENT
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE FIVE INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS TO THE PROGRAM CONTENT
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both Parties acknowledge and agree that with respect to Program Content licensed to Party B hereunder, Party B retains the right to create derivative works and shall own the copyright to these derivative works. If Party B obtains any Intellectual Property Right in respect of the Program Content during its use of the same, the ownership will be shared between the parties by 50%.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5.1 now explicitly grants Party B the right to create and own derivative works, as well as share intellectual property ownership, which directly contradicts the original text's statement that 'Party B shall not have any copyright or any other Intellectual Property Right.' The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, reinforces this contradiction because it grants Party B rights that the original text explicitly denied. This is a clear and direct conflict regarding intellectual property ownership, meeting the criteria for an in-text contradiction under the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
